title: atriplex latifolia subsp. triangularis
intro: Atriplex latifolia subsp. triangularis, also known as the broadleaf orache, is a subspecies of the Atriplex latifolia plant, characterized by its wide, triangular leaves and its ability to thrive in arid environments. The broadleaf orache has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Atriplex latifolia subsp. triangularis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and creams to promote skin health and wound healing.
